mainEntity.name: How is hearing loss measured for workers' comp in Utah after noise exposure?
mainEntity.text: How is hearing loss measured for workers' comp in Utah after noise exposure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah Code 34A-2-507 requires audiometric testing no sooner than 6 weeks after noise exposure ends, using ANSI-S3.6-1969 calibrated audiometers by qualified professionals to measure hearing threshold levels from 0 dB.

suggestedAnswer.text: Hearing loss for Utah workers' comp is quantified via audiograms performed at least 6 weeks post-exposure by medical experts using ANSI-standard equipment. This establishes permanent threshold shift for compensation. Employers may dispute tests; independent medical exams are common. Retain records and seek legal review to counter biased employer-provided tests. Proper measurement directly impacts benefit amounts.[4]

name: How Utah Measures Workplace Hearing Loss for Compensation
